description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with three stages: build, pages, and release, using docker:latest with the docker:dind service for Docker jobs and ruby:3.0 for the site build. In the build stage add a build:docker job that runs the shared before_script (docker info and docker login with $CI_JOB_TOKEN), then executes docker build -t $CURRENT_IMAGE_TAG . and docker push $CURRENT_IMAGE_TAG, uses the variables CURRENT_IMAGE_TAG, LATEST_IMAGE_TAG, DOCKER_DRIVER, and is manual. Also in the build stage add a build:site job that runs apt-get update, apt-get upgrade -y, apt-get install nodejs -y, gem install bundler --no-document, bundle update --bundler, bundle install with deployment options, caches the vendor directory under key vendor, builds the site with bundle exec jekyll build -d ./.public, and stores ./.public as an artifact. In the pages stage add a pages job that moves ./.public to ./public, publishes ./public as a pages artifact, sets the production environment with URL https://www.chillcoding.com, and runs only on the master branch. Also add a pages:review job that runs on all branches except master, moves ./.public to ./public, stores public as an artifact, and creates a review environment named review/$CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME with a URL to the job artifacts. Finally, in the release stage add a release:docker job that runs the shared before_script, pulls $CURRENT_IMAGE_TAG, tags it as $LATEST_IMAGE_TAG, pushes the latest tag, uses docker:dind, runs only on master, and is manual.
